sanskrit:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः।
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते॥
transliteration:
jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣyāmy aśeṣataḥ |
yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam avaśiṣyate ||
literal_meaning: Knowledge—unto you—I—along with—metaknowledge—this—I shall speak—without remainder; which—having known—not—here—again—anything else—to be known—remains.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a complete, no-stone-unturned map of reality: both how the mind constructs experience (phenomenal knowledge) and the awareness that observes it (noumenal knowledge). Once this dual insight is owned, the anxious search for more answers ends; the mind stops outsourcing wisdom.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative readiness—Arjuna’s mind is quiet enough to receive a full-spectrum explanation; the defensive need to debate or doubt is temporarily suspended.
trigger_scenario: A client on the verge of burnout who has tried every self-help hack finally hears the therapist say, "Let’s stop adding tools. Tonight I’ll give you the single frame that makes every other frame make sense."
anchor_text: Know the mind’s story and the silent watcher; then nothing remains to be known.
